'''''Republic'' 82''' is the eighty-second, penultimate issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]''. It was written by [[John Ostrander]], illustrated by [[Jan Duursema]], and first published on [[January 11]], [[2006]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. The issue features the story "'''Hidden Enemy Part Two'''," in which [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] faces the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] during the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]].

==Publisher's summary==
''A battle rages on the Wookiee homeworld of Kashyyyk. Victory is in sight for the clone armies of the Republic, led by Jedi Masters Yoda, Luminara Unduli, and Quinlan Vos, but none of them are aware that victory will lead to a betrayal that will change the galaxy forever. There is one chance to survive, and one course remaining to any who survive …''

''The fate of the Jedi Order is sealed, but a single Jedi may find a path to the future … or to the grave.''

==Plot summary==
{{Plot|comic}}

==Development==
''Republic 82'' was first published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[January 11]], [[2006]].<ref name="DarkHorse" /> In [[February]] [[2008]], the issue was reprinted for [[Hasbro]]'s ''Star Wars Comic Pack'' 41, which included action figures of Quinlan Vos and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Faie/Legends|Faie]].<ref name="Comic Pack">''Star Wars Comic Pack'' 41</ref>

==Continuity==
According to editor [[Randy Stradley]], ''Republic 82'' was originally planned to depict Quinlan Vos's death during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], adapting the character's scripted death in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. However, when that scene was cut from the film, it was decided that Vos would only be wounded in the comic.<ref name="Twitter">{{Twitter|Randy_Stradley|status/929489523444039680|[[Randy Stradley]]|quote=In George's original screenplay for RotS, there was a scene where Vos was killed, so we put it in the adaptation -- even though there was more we wanted to do with him. Later, we learned Vos's scene was cut (never filmed), so we decided he was only wounded.|archivefile=File:Twitter-StradleyVos.png|archivedate=20191205232917}}</ref>
